methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Causaly is a Series B-stage biotech and health software company that has raised $95M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, supplemented by 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two methodologies are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $95M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $180M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same-stage comparables: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M). The median across this set is $1.3B, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Causaly's revenue is not disclosed; however, the 3 public peers in this sector trade at a median 6.1x price-to-sales.

Triangulating these two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $153M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $740M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Causaly's revenue scale, unit economics, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set. The peer-median method carries particular weight given the proximity of comparable companies, though the substantial variance within the peer cohort ($600M–$3.3B) underscores the sensitivity of biotech valuations to differentiation and clinical/commercial trajectory.
